48	}
49	
50	const BASE_PREMIUMS: Record<string, number> = {
51	  sword: 100,
52	  amulet: 60,
53	  staff: 80,
54	  potion: 40,
55	  rune: 25,
56	  moonstone: 25,
57	};
58	
59	const INSURANCE_VALUES: Record<string, number> = {
60	  sword: 1000,
61	  amulet: 600,
62	  staff: 800,
63	  potion: 400,
64	  rune: 250,
65	  moonstone: 250,
66	};
67	
68	const COMPONENT_TYPES = new Set(["rune", "moonstone"]);
69	const DEDUCTIBLE = 100;
70	const PROCESSING_FEE = 5;
71	const BLOCK_SIZE = 3;
72	const BLOCK_PRICE = 60;
73	const CURSED_SURCHARGE_RATE = 0.5;
74	const HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_SURCHARGE_RATE = 0.3;
75	const HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_THRESHOLD = 5;
76	const FIRST_INSURANCE_SURCHARGE_RATE = 0.1;
77	const LOYALTY_DISCOUNT_RATE = 0.2;
78	const LOYALTY_YEARS_THRESHOLD = 2;
79	const FOLLOW_UP_DISCOUNT_RATE = 0.15;
80	const CLAIM_HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_THRESHOLD = 8;
81	const CLAIM_HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_REIMBURSEMENT_RATE = 0.5;
82	
83	const validateItemType = (type: string): void => {
84	  if (!(type in BASE_PREMIUMS)) {
85	    throw new Error(`Unknown item type: ${type}`);
86	  }
87	};
88	
89	const calculateItemSurcharges = (item: Item): number => {
90	  const base = BASE_PREMIUMS[item.type];
91	  let surcharge = 0;
92	  if (item.cursed) {
93	    surcharge += base * CURSED_SURCHARGE_RATE;
94	  }
95	  if (item.enchantment >= HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_THRESHOLD) {
96	    surcharge += base * HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_SURCHARGE_RATE;
97	  }
98	  return surcharge;
99	};
100	
101	const calculateComponentPremium = (componentCounts: Record<string, number>): number => {
102	  let total = 0;
103	  for (const [type, count] of Object.entries(componentCounts)) {
104	    total += count === BLOCK_SIZE ? BLOCK_PRICE : count * BASE_PREMIUMS[type];
105	  }
106	  return total;
107	};
108	
109	const calculatePolicyModifiers = (basePremium: number, customer: { yearsWithMHPCO: number }, quoteIndex: number): number => {
110	  const firstInsuranceSurcharge = basePremium * FIRST_INSURANCE_SURCHARGE_RATE;
111	  const loyaltyDiscount = customer.yearsWithMHPCO >= LOYALTY_YEARS_THRESHOLD ? basePremium * LOYALTY_DISCOUNT_RATE : 0;
112	  const followUpDiscount = quoteIndex > 0 ? basePremium * FOLLOW_UP_DISCOUNT_RATE : 0;
113	  return firstInsuranceSurcharge - loyaltyDiscount - followUpDiscount;
114	};
115	
116	const calculateQuotePremium = (items: Item[], customer: { yearsWithMHPCO: number }, quoteIndex: number): number => {
117	  let basePremium = 0;
118	  let itemSurcharges = 0;
119	  const componentCounts: Record<string, number> = {};
120	
121	  for (const item of items) {
122	    validateItemType(item.type);
123	    if (COMPONENT_TYPES.has(item.type)) {
124	      componentCounts[item.type] = (componentCounts[item.type] || 0) + 1;
125	    } else {
126	      basePremium += BASE_PREMIUMS[item.type];
127	      itemSurcharges += calculateItemSurcharges(item);
128	    }
129	  }
130	
131	  basePremium += calculateComponentPremium(componentCounts);
132	  const policyModifiers = calculatePolicyModifiers(basePremium, customer, quoteIndex);
133	  return Math.ceil(basePremium + itemSurcharges + policyModifiers) + PROCESSING_FEE;
134	};
135	
136	const CAP_MULTIPLIER = 2;
137	
138	const calculateInsuranceSum = (items: Item[]): number =>
139	  items.reduce((sum, item) => sum + INSURANCE_VALUES[item.type], 0);
140	
141	const validateClaimDamages = (damages: Damage[], policy: Policy): void => {
142	  const damageCounts: Record<string, number> = {};
143	  for (const damage of damages) {
144	    if (damage.amount < 0) {
145	      throw new Error(`Negative damage amount: ${damage.amount}`);
146	    }
147	    const policyItem = policy.items.find((i) => i.type === damage.itemType);
148	    if (!policyItem) {
149	      throw new Error(`Claim references item type not in policy: ${damage.itemType}`);
150	    }
151	    damageCounts[damage.itemType] = (damageCounts[damage.itemType] || 0) + 1;
152	  }
153	  for (const [itemType, count] of Object.entries(damageCounts)) {
154	    const policyCount = policy.items.filter((i) => i.type === itemType).length;
155	    if (count > policyCount) {
156	      throw new Error(`More damage entries for ${itemType} than policy covers`);
157	    }
158	  }
159	};
160	
161	const calculateDamageReimbursement = (damage: Damage, policyItem: Item): number => {
162	  const reimbursement = policyItem.enchantment >= CLAIM_HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_THRESHOLD
163	    ? damage.amount * CLAIM_HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_REIMBURSEMENT_RATE
164	    : damage.amount;
165	  return Math.max(0, reimbursement - DEDUCTIBLE);
166	};
167	
168	const calculateClaimPayout = (step: ClaimStep, policy: Policy): ClaimResult => {
169	  validateClaimDamages(step.incident.damages, policy);
170	
171	  let totalPayout = 0;
172	  for (const damage of step.incident.damages) {
173	    const policyItem = policy.items.find((i) => i.type === damage.itemType)!;
174	    totalPayout += calculateDamageReimbursement(damage, policyItem);
175	  }
176	
177	  totalPayout = Math.min(Math.floor(totalPayout), policy.remainingCap);
178	  policy.remainingCap -= totalPayout;
179	  return { payout: totalPayout, remainingCap: policy.remainingCap };
180	};
181	
182	export const processScenario = (scenario: Scenario): ScenarioResult => {
183	  let quoteCount = 0;
184	  const policies: Policy[] = [];
185	  const results = scenario.steps.map((step, index) => {
186	    if (step.op === "quote") {
187	      const premium = calculateQuotePremium(step.items, scenario.customer, quoteCount);
188	      const insuranceSum = calculateInsuranceSum(step.items);
189	      policies[index] = { items: step.items, insuranceSum, remainingCap: insuranceSum * CAP_MULTIPLIER };
190	      quoteCount++;
191	      return { premium };
192	    }
193	    return calculateClaimPayout(step, policies[step.policy]);
194	  });
195	  return { results };
196	};
197	"}]},"uuid":"f791d9e2-1337-4f81-a87e-20c5b378c072","timestamp":"2026-05-19T16:04:36.366Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"59961637-5a4b-45d7-bec6-891c6942edc5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"sdk-cli","cwd":"/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-19_15-23-51_claim-office-example-mapping_v6.3-no-pep_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-2","sessionId":"7292df97-d6b4-4d53-a7a4-b43927a56bda","version":"2.1.107","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"async-enchanting-sutton"}

content.text:
# TDD Green Phase

You are now in the **Green Phase** of TDD. Follow these instructions to make the failing test pass with MINIMAL code.

## Your Mission

1. Implement the minimal code necessary to make the failing test pass
2. Use the simplest possible solution (hardcoded values are acceptable)
3. Do not add features for future tests
4. Verify all tests pass
5. No optimization, no refactoring during Green phase

## Context: 

## Green Phase Rules

- **Minimal code only**: Just enough to pass the current test
- **Smallest change**: Make the smallest possible change
- **No future features**: Don't implement what future tests might need
- **Hardcoded returns are allowed**
- **Tests must pass**: Verify all tests are green
- **No refactoring during Green phase**: Save improvements for Refactor phase

## Process

### Step 1: Analyze the Failing Test

Identify:
- What input does the test provide?
- What output does it expect?
- What is the simplest way to produce that output?

### Step 2: Write Minimal Implementation

Implement only what's needed to make the current test pass:

```typescript
// For first test "should return 0 for empty input":
export const calculate = (input: string): number => {
  return 0;
};

// For second test "should return number for single input":
export const calculate = (input: string): number => {
  if (input === "") return 0;
  return parseInt(input);
};

// For third test "should add two numbers":
export const calculate = (input: string): number => {
  if (input === "") return 0;
  const numbers = input.split(",");
  if (numbers.length === 1) return parseInt(numbers[0]);
  return parseInt(numbers[0]) + parseInt(numbers[1]);
};
```

### Step 3: Run Tests

Run `pnpm test:unit:basic` and verify:
- Current test now passes ✅
- All previous tests still pass ✅

### Step 4: Verify No Over-Implementation

Check:
- ❓ Did I implement features for future tests? → Remove them
- ❓ Did I add logic not demanded by current test? → Remove it
- ❓ Did I optimize prematurely? → Simplify
- ❓ Did I refactor existing code? → Revert, save for Refactor phase

### Step 5: Report Completion

```
🟢 Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: [brief description of what was added]
**Result**: All tests now pass ([X] passing)

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
```

## Minimal Implementation Strategies

### Hardcoded Returns

```typescript
// Test: "should return 0 for empty input"
return 0;
```

### Simple Conditionals

```typescript
// Test: "should return number for single input"
if (input === "") return 0;
return parseInt(input);
```

### Generalization (only when forced by a new test)

```typescript
// Test: "should add multiple numbers"
return input.split(",").reduce((sum, n) => sum + parseInt(n), 0);
```

## Important Guidelines

### DO
- ✅ Write minimal code to make test pass
- ✅ Use hardcoded values when appropriate
- ✅ Take small steps
- ✅ Verify all tests pass

### DON'T
- ❌ Implement beyond what tests demand
- ❌ Add features for future tests
- ❌ Optimize prematurely
- ❌ Refactor during Green phase

## Completion

After completing Green phase, proceed to Refactor phase:

```
🟢 Green Phase Complete. Proceeding to Refactor phase.
```
